prompt: Develop a bounded, local-first arithmetic drill module for children ages 5-8. The module presents short, timed or untimed arithmetic questions (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) and accepts numeric input. Provide immediate visual and audio feedback on correctness, track session score and streak, and offer simple celebratory micro-rewards (sounds, animations, stickers) after correct answers or milestones. Include a mode selector to choose operation type and difficulty range. No cloud sync, no accounts, no ads. Prioritize clear, large touch targets and child-friendly UI.
preschool color quiz multiple choice children 3-6 identify colors

A learner wants to understand how Archimedes approximated pi using polygons, without reading a textbook. They need to see a circle with overlaid inscribed/circumscribed polygons, adjust the side count, and watch the perimeter converge toward circumference.

I need a personal citation reference manager that operates locally without cloud services. It should allow manual entry of citation records with smart field navigation, support batch import from standard formats (BibTeX, RIS, MEDLINE/SilverPlatter), and export citations in multiple formats. Include a bibliography generator that formats references according to common style templates and a print-friendly view for producing formatted reference lists. Keep scope bounded to individual personal use, not team collaboration.

A local-first multi-sport workout logging module. Users can quickly log workouts across different sports, view weekly overviews, see weekly/yearly totals, and filter their workout history by sport or criteria. Features an expandable notes field that can accept imported text. Minimal interface optimized for fast entry.

Create a bounded desktop automation module that connects to a specific analytical or data visualization desktop application, with embedded script templates, a built-in command reference for the target application's scripting API, batch file processing capabilities, and a local-first architecture. No cloud dependencies required.

You are DictCheck Local, a bounded local-first spell-check module. Given plain text input and optional custom dictionary file paths, scan the text against a system dictionary and any user-provided custom dictionaries to identify misspellings. Return a structured list of suspected misspellings with their positions, suggested corrections from the merged dictionary, and a flag for words that may be proper nouns or domain terms suitable for addition to a custom dictionary. Do not connect to external APIs or remote services. Operate entirely offline with local dictionary files.

A desktop application that loads guitar tablature files (GuitarPro formats, ASCII tab), parses the notation and timing data, and plays back the tablature with adjustable tempo, looping capabilities for sections, and navigation controls. The application should allow users to slow down playback for practice, loop specific measures or sections, and visually follow along with the scrolling tablature.
User wants to quickly retrieve verified facts about Texas geography, famous figures, landmarks, or historical events in an offline-capable, cross-linked reference format

Create a local-first mobile glossary app for learning agricultural machinery terminology. Include bilingual term pairs (English-French), audio pronunciation for each term, and annotated diagrams showing machine parts. Terms organized by machine area (e.g., cutter head, feed rolls, discharge elbow). Use IndexedDB for offline storage and Service Worker for offline access.
User seeks Arkansas-specific reference information (cities, people, places, events, history)

Create a bounded self-paced language learning module for Esperanto grammar and pronunciation. The module should contain: 1) Progressive grammar lesson units covering fundamentals (articles, noun cases, verb conjugation, sentence structure), 2) A searchable grammar and usage reference section, 3) Integrated text-to-speech pronunciation practice for key phrases and vocabulary, 4) Vocabulary checklists with audio validation, 5) Progress tracking that persists locally. The module must work offline and store all user progress and settings locally.
Generate a multiplication worksheet set with customizable parameters and a paired answer key for teacher distribution.

Design a bounded local-first research knowledge base module that: (1) ingests plain text and markdown files from local folders, (2) allows inline annotation/marginalia on each record, (3) provides fast full-text search across all imported content, (4) organizes records into project-based collections with tags, (5) supports embedding images and attachments, (6) offers sortable views by date/title/custom fields. The module should be sovereign and offline-capable, suitable for individual researchers collecting correspondence and reference materials.

Create a standalone HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript that functions as a fan trivia quiz. It should load questions from a const QUESTIONS array in the script, display one question at a time with 4 multiple-choice buttons, highlight correct/incorrect after each answer or at the end, show a final score summary with percentage, and provide a 'Play Again' button to reset. Design should be clean and responsive, suitable for embedding in a fan community site. Include a sample question set of at least 10 questions about a specific TV show or movie franchise.

Design a local-first spaced repetition flashcard application for language vocabulary acquisition. Users can create, organize, and edit vocabulary decks with categories, add term/translation pairs, customize review intervals using spaced repetition scheduling (FSRS-style), and track learning progress over time. The module should handle 10,000+ cards efficiently, support deck nesting, allow bulk import/export, and provide detailed review statistics without requiring cloud connectivity.

Create a self-contained temperament self-assessment quiz as a single HTML file using vanilla JavaScript and CSS. The quiz should: 1) Display an intro explaining the 4-temperament model (based on Hippocrates) before starting. 2) Present 12 multiple-choice questions, each with 4 answer options mapping to the four temperaments. 3) Use radio buttons for single-answer selection per question with clear Next/Previous navigation. 4) Calculate scores across all four temperament categories client-side. 5) Display results showing the primary and secondary temperament types with a brief personalized interpretation paragraph. 6) Include a Retake Quiz button. Style with a clean, approachable design appropriate for a personal development tool.

Design a local-first bounded module for kanji character lookup and reference. The module should allow users to search kanji by radical components, stroke count, or drawing input. It should display character details including readings, meanings, and stroke order animations. Include local storage for saving favorite kanji to study lists. Avoid expanding into full vocabulary dictionary or AI translation features. Keep it as a single-purpose reference tool.
import research notes from external files or clipboard, link to bibliography entries, search quickly with find/find-next, sort by date/tag/author, export as editable text for word processors

Write a bounded, condition-specific patient education reference module for local-first use. The module should cover prevention guidance, symptom reference, diagnosis information, treatment options, and regional context. Structure it as a navigable knowledge base without requiring network connectivity or user accounts. Prioritize clear, action-oriented sections that patients or caregivers can reference independently.

Create a bounded vocabulary learning module for SAT test preparation that supports at least 3 study modes: (1) text-based flashcards with definitions and example sentences, (2) audio pronunciation with phonetic spelling, and (3) visual context cards with images or diagrams illustrating word usage. Include a spaced repetition scheduler that presents words due for review based on user performance, a progress tracker showing mastery percentage per vocabulary set, and a quiz mode that tests word meaning through multiple choice and fill-in-the-blank formats. The module must function offline and store all vocabulary data locally on the user's device.

Create a local-first animal classification quiz module for elementary science students. The module presents one animal at a time (name or image) and asks users to classify it as mammal, fish, bird, reptile, or amphibian. After each selection, show immediate feedback (correct/incorrect with the right answer), then advance to the next question. Track session score and total questions answered. No user accounts required—no server dependency. Runs in-browser or as standalone app. Include at least 30 diverse animal examples covering all five categories.

Design a privacy-respecting, local-first personal vehicle maintenance log module that lets users add vehicles, record service entries with date/mileage/cost/type/notes, set maintenance reminders based on time or mileage intervals, and view chronological service history per vehicle.

Build a Vermont Local Guide bounded module that lets users browse cities/towns, view school listings with details, discover points of interest, and filter by county. Include categories for educational institutions, municipal locations, and regional attractions with basic contact info and descriptions.
An adaptive multiplication fact fluency trainer module that presents timed practice problems, adapts difficulty based on mastery, tracks progress across sessions, and provides immediate feedback.

Generate a bounded single-artist music identification quiz module for Chuck Berry. Module must include: (a) 15-20 playable audio clips from his discography, (b) multiple-choice guess interface with 4 options per clip, (c) instant answer reveal with track metadata, (d) running score and streak counter, (e) compact reference section with 2-paragraph bio and chronological track list. Local-first architecture with offline clip caching. Audio clips should be 8-12 seconds each, sampled from identifiable song sections (riffs, verses, bridges). Difficulty should be calibrated to intermediate listener level.
I am a microbiology student studying GI tract organism identification. I want to practice interpreting growth patterns on selective agars to identify common enteric bacteria.

You are a PowerShell syntax quick-reference module. Provide concise command syntax lookup with parameter details, short working examples, and common usage patterns for the requested cmdlet or concept.

Design and implement a bounded, local-first gradebook module called 'GradeTrack' that lets teachers (1) manage a student roster with name/ID, (2) define graded assignments or projects each with a weight percentage, (3) input grades as letters (A-F with +/-) or raw scores, (4) configure the grading scale (e.g., A=93-100, A-=90-92), (5) automatically calculate weighted course averages, (6) add per-student comments for report cards, (7) generate student-facing report cards in printable format, and (8) generate registrar summary exports (CSV/spreadsheet). Prioritize privacy and local data ownership—no cloud sync or user accounts required. Include simple data backup/restore. The module should be a single self-contained application or library, not part of a larger LMS.

I want to run a bounded sci-fi RPG scenario using a self-contained digital module. I need structured player character creation materials, a scenario with setting context and scene hooks, GM quick-reference notes for running the session, and printable character sheets for at-table use. The module should be local-first, editable, and work offline—nothing that requires account registration or cloud dependency. What's the minimal viable structure for this?

Build a micro audio engine module for applications that provides playback of sound assets with independent per-channel volume (amplitude) control during playback and output routing to mono, left, right, or stereo modes. The module should be embeddable as a library/plugin and include reference examples for common application frameworks.
I want to practice geography knowledge with a quiz module that presents questions about world places and provides feedback
channel partner needs to learn how to explain technical hardware product features and benefits to end customers with confidence and accuracy

You are helping a traveler explore adventure activities at a destination (British Columbia) before arriving to decide which activities to prioritize. The module should work offline, present activities by category, provide brief visual/audio previews, and let users capture favorites to a mobile reference list.
Teacher provides vocabulary term list (optionally with definitions), selects card count and layout preferences, triggers generation

Design a bounded UI behavior module called 'ContextualHelp' that provides tooltip and popover functionality for interface controls. The module should: (1) Bind help content (text, styled text, media) to buttons, fields, and controls via data attributes or API, (2) Position tooltips near reference elements using smart positioning logic, (3) Support show/hide/toggle commands with configurable triggers (hover, focus, click), (4) Include install/setup helpers for easy integration, (5) Return errors as simple sentences when misconfigured. Target a lightweight, framework-agnostic implementation suitable for embedding in larger applications. Include example usage showing installation in a stack and basic command syntax.
A teacher wants to teach earth science concepts using interactive visual aids with a printable model component for hands-on classroom activity

A personal reference database as a single HTML file—local-first, portable, no server required. Stores discrete entries (like vocabulary words, quotes, contacts, notes) each with title, content, tags, and metadata. Supports bulk import from JSON or CSV files. Includes full-text search and tag-based filtering. User can create new entries, edit existing ones, search the full corpus, and export the database for backup.
catalog my personal music collection (vinyl records, CDs, cassettes) with artist, album, track lists, condition notes and cover reference images

You are building a Klingon bilingual dictionary and transliteration module called "pIqaD Lookup". The module lets users: 1) Search for Klingon terms by Latin transliteration or English definition; 2) View detailed entries with part of speech, definition, usage examples, and cultural notes; 3) Convert text bidirectionally between Okrandian transliteration, xifan hol scheme, and pIqaD script (using Unicode Private Use Area mapping). Include a standalone conversion tool for quick transliteration without a dictionary lookup. Keep it bounded: no language learning exercises, no community features, no broader Star Trek reference suite. Focus purely on lookup and conversion.

Create a bounded, local-first arithmetic speed drill module. The module should present timed arithmetic problems (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division), accept numeric input, provide instant correctness feedback, track session scores, persist historical performance data, and allow configuration of problem types and difficulty levels. Prioritize speed and simplicity over gamification features. All data remains local. Output the complete working module.

Design an embedded help module for a desktop animation/design tool that enables new users to learn core features through interactive walkthroughs, provides contextual guidance when users hover or click help icons, and offers a searchable quick reference for advanced features. Module should support progressive disclosure - basic quickstart for beginners, detailed walkthroughs for medium users, reference docs for advanced users.

Design and implement a bounded elementary math drill module for fraction-to-visual matching. The module should: (1) Display a written fraction (e.g., '3/4') as the prompt; (2) Present 4 visual options showing different fractions as circle or bar models; (3) Accept user selection and provide immediate correctness feedback; (4) Track session progress (items completed, accuracy, current streak); (5) Support difficulty levels based on denominator range and visual complexity; (6) Use local storage for progress persistence; (7) Render clean, child-friendly visuals using SVG or canvas. Focus on the matching mechanic only - not a general fraction explorer or calculator.

Create a bounded local-first desktop app called LocalQuiz Builder that enables users to create and take multiple-choice practice tests for one subject area. The app should work entirely offline, storing all question banks and quiz data locally on the user's device. Include a question editor for adding/editing multiple-choice items with customizable answer options, a test assembler for selecting questions into a quiz, a practice mode that presents questions and provides instant feedback, and a simple results tracker. The data format should be human-readable JSON for easy user modification and backup.

Create a bounded study module for FCC Technician Ham Radio license exam preparation. The module must: contain the complete current Technician question pool in machine-readable format; enable randomized or sequential quiz sessions presenting multiple-choice questions one at a time; provide instant right/wrong validation with answer explanations after each response; track progress showing mastery status for each question; operate fully offline without accounts or network connectivity. Scope is strictly limited to Technician class license only.

Build a self-contained interactive grammar module with: 1) a home screen showing the 8 parts of speech as clickable cards; 2) individual concept screens with definition, example sentence, and a 'try it' mini-quiz (3-4 questions per part); 3) immediate visual feedback on answers (correct/incorrect with explanation); 4) a simple progress indicator showing which parts have been reviewed; 5) subtle fade/slide transitions between screens. Use vanilla JavaScript, CSS animations, and localStorage for progress persistence. No external APIs required.

Write a bounded local-first haiku generator module called 'VerseFragment'. It generates 5-7-5 syllable haikus using user-provided vocabulary lists. Include editable word banks with syllable counts, a generator that composes random combinations respecting the 5-7-5 structure, and iterative output history. The module runs entirely client-side with no external API dependencies. Prioritize privacy, minimal load time, and the whimsical creative spirit of the original HyperCard stack.

Create a bounded reference module for looking up structured facts about California: cities, counties, geographic features, notable organizations, demographics, and points of interest. Include category-based navigation and entity pages with linked references.

>>> |>_USER_REQUEST_<<< :: I need a bounded local-first desktop math utility that combines fraction arithmetic with prime number operations. It should handle: (1) fraction operations (add/subtract/multiply/divide two fractions, simplify results), (2) GCD and LCM calculations for two integers, (3) primality checking for a single number, (4) prime factorization of any integer up to 10 trillion, and (5) generating primes within a given range or count. The UI should have mode selection, numeric input fields, operation controls, and clear result display. All computation must run entirely client-side with no network dependency. Write the SPEC.md and then implement the complete application. |>_SPECIFICATION_<<< :: # FractionPrime Calculator - Specification ## 1. Project Overview - **Project Name**: FractionPrime Calculator - **Type**: Desktop utility application (local-first) - **Core Functionality**: Combined fraction arithmetic and prime number theory calculator - **Target Users**: Students, educators, developers needing quick math computations offline ## 2. UI/UX Specification ### Layout Structure - Single window application (800x600 default, resizable min 600x500) - Header: app title and mode tabs - Main content: input area + operation controls + results - Footer: version info, optional history toggle ### Visual Design - **Color Palette**: - Background: #1a1a2e (dark navy) - Surface: #16213e (slightly lighter) - Primary accent: #0f3460 (deep blue) - Action: #e94560 (coral red) - Text primary: #eaeaea - Text secondary: #a0a0a0 - Success: #4ecca3 (mint green) - **Typography**: - Font: "JetBrains Mono", "Consolas", monospace - Heading: 18px bold - Body: 14px regular - Numbers/Results: 16px medium - **Spacing**: 8px base unit; 16px component gaps - **Visual Effects**: subtle box-shadow on cards, smooth tab transitions ### Components - **Mode Tabs**: Fractions | GCD-LCM | Prime Check | Factorize | Generate - **Input Fields**: Labeled numeric inputs with validation - **Operation Buttons**: Primary action buttons per mode - **Results Panel**: Formatted output with optional step display - **History Sidebar** (toggle): last 10 calculations ## 3. Functionality Specification ### Mode: Fractions - Inputs: Fraction A (numerator, denominator), Fraction B (numerator, denominator) - Operations: Add (+), Subtract (-), Multiply (×), Divide (÷), Simplify - Output: Resulting fraction in lowest terms, decimal approximation - Validation: denominators cannot be zero ### Mode: GCD-LCM - Inputs: Integer A, Integer B - Output: GCD (Euclidean algorithm), LCM - Display: Calculation steps optional ### Mode: Prime Check - Input: Single integer - Output: "Prime" / "Composite" + if composite, smallest factor ### Mode: Factorize - Input: Integer (1 to 10 trillion) - Output: Prime factorization as array, factor tree representation ### Mode: Generate Primes - Input: Range (min, max) OR Count (n primes from start) - Output: Array of primes, count, largest prime generated ### Data Handling - All math computed in TypeScript (BigInt for large numbers) - LocalStorage for history persistence only - No network calls ### Edge Cases - Zero denominators: show error, prevent calculation - Negative numbers: handle sign in fractions, reject for prime ops - Numbers exceeding safe integer: use BigInt - Empty inputs: disable calculate button ## 4. Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All five modes accessible and functional - [ ] Fraction results always reduced to lowest terms - [ ] Prime factorization handles numbers up to 10 trillion - [ ] History persists across sessions - [ ] Works completely offline - [ ] Responsive layout at minimum 600x500 - [ ] No console errors during normal operation

I need a minimal two-option vocabulary scenario quiz module. It should let me add scenario items (each with a scenario description and two answer choices), present them one at a time to a learner, capture which option they choose, optionally play a sound cue on transition, and let me export or review the response data.

Build a mental math speed drill module where users complete 25 arithmetic problems under time pressure. Include configurable difficulty (operations: add/subtract/multiply/divide), timed session tracking with millisecond precision, progress thermometer, local leaderboard storing personal best times per difficulty, and optional silent mode for faster execution.

You are building a single-artist audio recognition quiz module. The module should: (1) Present short audio clips (5-15 seconds) of Bo Diddley songs, (2) Accept user guesses via text input or button selection, (3) Provide immediate feedback with answer reveal, (4) Track cumulative score across sessions, (5) Include a compact biographical panel about Bo Diddley that unlocks or highlights after correct identifications. Keep scope tight—10-15 well-chosen tracks maximum. Use local file storage for clips and scores. No network dependency required for core quiz loop.

Create a bounded self-paced electronics learning module for absolute beginners (ages 10+, educators, hobbyists) that teaches practical circuit building and microcontroller basics through guided project-based lessons. The module should include: a main landing hub with clear pathway navigation, reference cards for basic components (resistors, capacitors, LEDs, switches), foundational circuit lessons, introductory Arduino or Raspberry Pi projects, and a project notebook for documenting builds. Kit-compatible with common beginner electronics sets. Focus on hands-on learning with minimal theory-first approach.

Create a bounded cultural heritage exhibit browser module. The module should enable users to browse curated educational exhibits about a specific culture (such as Celtic history) across multiple subject areas. Features: topic-based exhibit sections with media-rich overview pages, category navigation between subjects (arts, music, language, mythology, religion, social studies, archaeology, history), image galleries, artifact details, and educational descriptions. The interface should support non-linear exploration with thematic image tiles and detailed exhibit views. Scope: single culture/topic focus, not a broad suite. The module stores exhibit data locally and provides an immersive, educational browsing experience without requiring server connectivity for core content.

Design a bounded, local-first geography learning module that combines interactive reference maps with embedded factual content and an auto-generated quiz feature. The module should allow users to click on map regions to reveal facts, navigate from continent views to country/subdivision detail views, and generate quizzes from the factual content itself. Include basic progress tracking for quiz scores. The module should function fully offline with bundled data.

Create an Obsidian vault for MST3K fandom reference and personal collection tracking. Include: episode guide with season/episode metadata, FAQ section, glossary of terms, collection tracker with watch status fields, lyrics indexed by episode, game rules for MST Poker and drinking games. Use Dataview for queries across episodes. Keep it local-first, no cloud sync required. Module should support quick lookup and personal cataloging.

I want to practice reading Labanotation. Show me the Macarena phrase from the library. Let me step through measure by measure and tap any symbol to understand what it means. I'll try to describe the movement before seeing the explanation.

Design a bounded local-first map labeling exercise tool called GeoStudy Map Labeler. The tool should provide blank map templates for world regions (e.g., US states, South American countries, European countries), display a word bank of geographical features/places, and allow users to click or drag labels onto the correct map locations. Include progressive hints that guide the user through the exercise. The finished annotated map should exportable as PNG. Prefer a single HTML file with embedded SVG map data that works entirely offline.

Generate a single-page early reading lesson module called 'Freda Fed Fred' for beginning readers (ages 5-7). The module should display the sentence 'Freda fed Fred' with supporting cartoon illustrations. Include: (1) the full illustrated story card with large readable text, (2) a play button that triggers human-recorded narration of the sentence (not text-to-speech), (3) tap-to-hear pronunciation for each individual word, (4) simple next/complete navigation. No user accounts, no external links, no gamification beyond basic progress tracking. Keep it focused and distraction-free.

You are a local-first regional reference knowledge base for Maryland. Maintain structured pages for: (1) cities and places with key facts, (2) historic people and biographies, (3) interesting local facts and figures. Support hierarchical navigation and search. Keep it lightweight, self-contained, and easy to maintain.
local sovereign job lead tracking module with per-lead activity history, searchable lead list, and simple export

Create a local-first drill module for toddlers/preschoolers that: (1) presents a letter or number, (2) plays its pronunciation audio, (3) prompts the child to tap/click the matching character, (4) provides positive audio/visual feedback, (5) tracks progress through a small sequence of 5-10 items. Keep interface minimal, use large touch-friendly targets, include gentle error handling.

Start a pediatric diagnostic case: Present the patient history and physical findings, then progressively order diagnostic tests and make treatment decisions while tracking costs. Submit your final diagnosis and therapy, then review the debrief with differential diagnosis and rationale.

Create a Map Symbol Literacy Quiz module that helps children learn to read common urban map symbols through interactive quiz cards. The module should include: 1) A reference screen showing 12 common map symbols (school, hospital, park, police, fire, library, church, restaurant, hotel, gas station, bus stop, parking) with their meanings, 2) A quiz mode presenting map excerpts where users tap/click to identify highlighted symbols, 3) Immediate feedback showing correct/incorrect with simple animations, 4) A score tracker displaying right answers, wrong answers, and percentage, 5) Randomized question order to prevent pattern memorization, 6) A brief instruction screen accessible from any point. Target age is 8-10 years. Use child-friendly colors, large clickable areas, and encouraging language. Keep it as a single self-contained HTML file.

A minimalist fraction missing-numerator drill module: generates randomized fraction problems where denominator is shown and target equivalent fraction requires finding numerator. User enters answer, module validates, tracks score and progress, allows reset. Single HTML file, no external dependencies, stores session in localStorage.

Create a bounded local-first flashcard module that presents division problems (1÷1 through 12÷12), reveals answers on user demand, tracks progress through the deck, and stores all state locally with zero external accounts or cloud dependencies.

Design and implement a bounded study module for chemistry students to learn and practice common ion names, formulas, and charges. The module should include: (1) a searchable reference list of common monatomic and polyatomic ions organized by charge; (2) a flashcard mode where users flip cards to reveal ion formulas or charges; (3) a quiz mode with multiple choice or fill-in questions; (4) progress tracking showing mastery level; (5) immediate feedback with explanations. Keep the scope focused on common ions only - do not expand into full periodic table or general chemistry curriculum. Design for mobile-first use with spaced repetition principles.

I need a simple, local-first tool where I can paste 20 vocabulary words and instantly generate multiple different printable worksheets from them. I want to choose templates like vocabulary charts, Frayer models, fill-in-the-blank sentences, matching exercises, and word searches. The output should be clean, print-ready PDFs I can hand out to students.

User needs to practice and solve basic geometry problems (triangles, circles, squares, rectangles) and algebra problems (linear equations, quadratic equations) with interactive exercises and concept reinforcement

A simple browser-based metronome tool for musicians practicing guitar or piano. Users select from 6-8 preset rhythm patterns (basic rock, pop, jazz, bossa nova, waltz, etc.), adjust tempo from 40-240 BPM, tap to set tempo, and start/stop playback. Audio uses Web Audio API for clean drum sounds. No accounts, no cloud, no social sharing - just a focused practice tool.

Create a bounded phonogram recognition practice module for foundational literacy intervention. The module should present English phonograms (e.g., 'sh', 'th', 'ch', 'ai', 'ea', 'ou') with audio/visual cues and ask learners to identify or select the corresponding sound. Include immediate feedback for correct/incorrect responses, maintain progress data per learner, and organize phonogram sets by systematic progression. Avoid expanding into broader literacy instruction - keep this focused strictly on phonogram-to-sound recognition drilling.
Generate a one-page reference card for [STATE], including population, capital, notable cities, state bird/flower/song, and 2-3 key geographic facts.

You are building a local-first Proportion Analysis Module for beauty and styling consultants. The module captures 30 body measurements, computes comparative analysis against standard beauty proportions and ratios (such as waist-to-hip, shoulder-to-hip, vertical ratios), and generates a client summary with discussion prompts. The output should include: (1) raw measurements captured in structured form, (2) computed ratios with reference comparisons, (3) a client-facing summary of key findings, and (4) consultant-only discussion points for follow-up. Keep the module bounded to beauty/styling consultation contexts—not medical diagnosis. Output the complete module specification in JSON format with clear separation between the measurement schema, analysis logic, and output templates.

Build a local-first code snippet manager with an integrated script testing playground. Users can save, tag, search, and organize reusable code snippets. Include a personal "save for later" collection, a code scratchpad for testing snippets, bulk find/replace across the library, and built-in reference documentation. Prioritize offline use, fast search, and easy snippet reuse.

You are a bounded scientific calculator module for engineering and technical workflows. Provide precise calculations using trigonometric functions (sine, cosine, tangent), logarithmic functions (natural log, log base 10), exponential functions, and factorials. Include a constants library with common physical constants (speed of light, Avogadro number, gas constant, gravitational acceleration, Planck constant). Support degree/radian mode toggle. Display results in decimal, scientific notation, and fraction approximation formats. Allow keyboard input and maintain a calculation history buffer. Do not expand into symbolic algebra, graphing, or programming - stay bounded as a single-purpose calculation tool.
user seeks Virginia historical sites, battlefields, or local history by city or theme

Create a minimal Delaware Places Reference module. Include: (1) An index page listing all Delaware municipalities organized alphabetically with population tier badges; (2) Individual place detail pages showing population, county, geography, and 3-5 key facts; (3) A simple header with search/filter capability. Keep the design clean, text-forward, and reference-focused. No business listings, reviews, or interactive maps required—static reference only. The scope is bounded to Delaware only.

Design a local-first mobile module that provides offline street and landmark lookup for Carmel-by-the-Sea. Users can search street names, find points of interest, and orient using local reference data - all without network dependency.
scenario-based quiz game teacher authoring customizable questions classroom medieval RPG encounter flow

Develop a standalone interactive training module that teaches the user how a 2-stroke engine works. The module must contain three bounded sections: (1) a labeled reference view showing all major engine components with interactive labels explaining each part's function; (2) a step-by-step animated or stepped visualization of the 2-stroke operating cycle (intake/compression, power/exhaust) showing how components interact during each phase; (3) a knowledge check quiz with multiple-choice questions that test understanding of component functions and the operating cycle. The module should be self-contained, run offline or in-browser without external dependencies, and provide immediate feedback on quiz answers.

Build a bounded interactive quiz module that displays a sequence of 5 questions one at a time. Each question should have 4 multiple choice options. Use Framer Motion for smooth slide transitions between questions. Track correct answers in local component state. After the final question, show a summary screen with score and feedback. Design and build a local-first note-reading drill app. Core loop: display a random note on a musical staff (user-selected clef), user clicks the corresponding piano key within a configurable time limit, system scores and tracks the response. Required settings: time-per-note (3-30 seconds), note types (whole notes only, or include sharps, or include flats, or both), clef selection. Data stays local - simple JSON or SQLite store for mistake counts per note. No cloud, no accounts, no social features. UI should show staff prominently, piano keyboard below, timer countdown visible, current score displayed. Progressive difficulty not required - the adaptivity comes from weighting wrong answers, not from overall difficulty ramping.

Create a bounded, local-first technical writing style guide training module that teaches users how to apply consistent writing conventions. The module should include: 1) Explanations of core style guide concepts from established guides (Google Developer Documentation Style Guide, GitLab Technical Writing Fundamentals, DigitalOcean conventions); 2) Interactive exercises where users apply specific rules to sample technical content; 3) Practice checkpoints that assess ability to identify and correct convention violations; 4) A reference card summarizing key rules for quick lookup. Focus on practical application rather than comprehensive theory. Module should be self-contained and work offline.

Create a bounded module that provides a single global hotkey to toggle the currently active window between its normal position and a parked position at the bottom of the primary screen, preserving the window's exact position and size for instant restoration. The module should be triggered by a configurable modifier+key combination (default: Shift+Ctrl+W), work across all applications, and maintain position history in local storage for instant restore.
teacher spelling practice custom word lists printable study sheets grade-level
Query American English style rule or convention
User wants to practice English pronunciation
curated ecology book list with ISBN price ordering metadata

Create an interactive single-topic math exploration module focused on the concept of Pi. The module should include an interactive visualization area where users can explore circular calculations and Pi relationships. Feature a context-sensitive help panel that updates based on the current exploration state. Include a learner mode toggle that prevents accidental changes to settings. The module should support guided step-through of the history of Pi calculations from Archimedes to modern computation. Target audience: learners seeking structured, protected exploration of a specific mathematical concept.
Instructional content, learning objectives, quiz questions, and remediation logic

Generate a bounded HTML/CSS/JS module for a 'Stargazer Narratives' astronomy lesson. The module should display 5-6 constellation cards, each showing: (1) a constellation image/illustration, (2) the mythology story associated with that constellation, (3) 2-3 key astronomy facts. Include navigation between cards and a progress indicator. Use local-first data—no external APIs.

You are building a modern SerialPort SDK module. This module provides a developer toolkit for integrating serial and USB-serial device communication into desktop applications. The module includes: (1) Core serial communication library handling port discovery, configuration, read/write operations, and flow control; (2) Language-specific runtime bindings for Python, Node.js, and optionally Rust; (3) Comprehensive documentation covering API reference, configuration options, and troubleshooting; (4) Example source implementations demonstrating common device integration patterns; (5) Optional device driver utilities for USB-serial bridge chipsets. The module must support cross-platform operation (Windows, macOS, Linux), provide a clean synchronous and asynchronous API, handle common serial protocols (RS-232, CDC), and enable developers to quickly add hardware communication capabilities to their applications without building from scratch. The module is a bounded, single-purpose developer tool focused exclusively on serial device integration, not a general-purpose communication suite.

Design a bounded music terminology reference module that works offline and includes: a searchable index of over 2600 musical terms with instant lookup, detailed definition views showing term origin and usage context, bidirectional cross-references linking related terms (e.g., tempo markings to rhythm terms, instrument families to playing techniques), and export functionality allowing users to save definitions as text, JSON, or formatted documents. Prioritize local-first architecture for use without internet connectivity.

Create a bounded, local-first web module for K-2 dual language reading and vocabulary practice. Include four short children's stories presented in parallel English and Spanish. Each story page should allow learners to click on highlighted vocabulary words to see the translation in the other language. Add a simple quiz mode where students match English words to their Spanish equivalents. Include a printable handout view showing each story with its vocabulary list. The module should work entirely offline in a single HTML file with no external dependencies.

Create a single-file Python Streamlit app for personal weather tracking. Requirements: (1) Search and add cities via OpenWeather API with autocomplete, (2) Display saved cities in a list/grid showing current temp, conditions, and timestamp, (3) Persist saved cities in local JSON file, (4) Interactive line chart showing temperature history for a selected city, (5) Manual refresh button and auto-refresh toggle, (6) Toggle between Celsius/Fahrenheit. Use st.session_state for reactivity and caching with st.cache_data. Keep it a single bounded module focused on personal multi-city weather monitoring.
